response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented generalist with strong testing culture and broad full-stack experience, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. His C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, far from the required 8 years; his primary depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. His large-scale infrastructure experience (~2 years of low-latency microservices at Sociomantic) falls short of the 5-year bar, and he has no formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers, which this technical-lead position centers on.
strengths: Strong testing and quality mindset — TDD advocate, author of test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and testing workshop instructor
Real high-throughput systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of requests/second, low-latency async I/O)
Demonstrated mentoring inclination via workshops, courses, and community talks, plus long-running ownership of an open-source platform
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ minimum — only ~7 months of professional C/C++ work, with primary depth in TypeScript and DLang
Limited large-scale infrastructure and cloud architecture depth (~2 years), well under the 5-year requirement for distributed systems
No formal technical-lead or people-leadership role; leadership evidence is informal (workshops, side projects) rather than cross-team strategy ownership
